title: Crispy Citrus Egyptian Brussels Sprouts Curry
description: A bright, citrus-infused curry that turns humble Brussels sprouts into a crispy, crowd‑pleasing Egyptian-inspired dish.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and let them sizzle for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for another minute.
Add the halved Brussels sprouts, stirring to coat them in the spice mixture.
Season with turmeric, coriander, garam masala, and salt, stirring well.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring to a gentle simmer, and cook for 10–12 minutes, allowing the sprouts to soften and the broth to reduce slightly.
Increase the heat to medium‑high, then drizzle a little more oil and stir in the lemon juice and orange zest, letting the sprouts develop a crispy edge as they brown, about 5 minutes.
Once the sprouts are tender yet crispy and the sauce has thickened, taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Transfer to a serving dish, sprinkle chopped cilantro on top, and serve hot with rice or flatbread.
